title: scrophularia sambucifolia subsp. mellifera
intro: Scrophularia sambucifolia subsp. mellifera, also known as honey-flowered figwort, is a plant species within the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and inflammation, as well as its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and respiratory infections, as well as having antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, Scrophularia sambucifolia subsp. mellifera is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in the production of salves and creams for topical application.
